task.name: Plan Shape Compact
task.description: Review and compact a plan shape.md into future-agent signal only
task.objective: Review one plan shape document and compact it in place so future execution agents see only decisions, findings, constraints, unresolved questions, and required references. Remove work-log noise without deleting signal.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
Never treat compaction as summarization. Preserve all execution-shaping signal.
User inputs are signal. Keep them, with concise summarization allowed.
User questions are noise unless they triggered a plan change, decision, finding, constraint, or unresolved question.
When unsure whether content is signal or noise, keep it and tighten wording.
Do not write the compacted file until the user approves the proposed rewrite.

substep.title: Get Shape Path
substep.action: If the user provided a shape file path, use it.
If the user provided a plan directory, locate `shape.md` inside that directory.
substep.ask: If no target is clear, ask for the `shape.md` path or plan directory.

substep.title: Validate Target
substep.action: Confirm the target exists and is a markdown file.
Read the entire target before analyzing.

substep.title: Build Signal Ledger
substep.action: Classify every substantive section or entry as KEEP, DROP, or REWRITE.
KEEP scope boundaries, exclusions, user inputs, decisions, rationale, constraints, findings, unresolved questions, source-of-truth notes, required reference paths, and user-reserved judgment.
DROP routine task completions, status updates, "what I did" narration, stale progress notes, duplicate context, process chatter, and user questions that did not change future execution.
REWRITE mixed entries by removing log sentences and preserving the user input, decision, finding, constraint, or implication.

substep.title: Protect Against Over-Compaction
substep.action: For each DROP item, verify it has no downstream decision value.
Never drop user inputs. Summarize them only when the summary preserves the execution-shaping meaning.
Never drop rationale just because it is verbose. Tighten rationale instead.
Never drop a rejected alternative if it prevents a future agent from repeating a bad path.
Flag any proposed deletion of a decision, rationale, constraint, or unresolved question as a review risk.

substep.title: Preserve Structure
substep.action: Preserve the document's useful top-level structure unless it is duplicative or misleading.
Keep `Original Shaping`, `User Inputs`, `Standards Applied`, `Decisions and Discoveries`, and `References` when present.
Remove explicit work-log sections unless they contain decisions or findings; move preserved signal into the closest appropriate section.

substep.title: Rewrite for Agents
substep.action: Write compact entries as direct instructions or findings future agents can act on.
Use concrete subjects, sources, and implications. Avoid generic summaries.
Keep enough rationale for a future agent to understand why the decision exists.
Do not add a compaction log to the shape document.

substep.title: Present Proposed Edit
substep.action: Show the user a concise review summary: target path, approximate size change, KEEP/DROP/REWRITE counts, and any review risks.
List the top removed noise categories and the signal categories preserved.
If the compacted draft removes more than half of non-boilerplate content, explicitly flag high compaction and ask for confirmation.

substep.title: Request Approval
substep.ask: Choose one: (a) apply in place, (b) show the full compacted draft first, or (c) stop without editing.

substep.title: Apply Approved Rewrite
substep.action: If approved, replace the target file with the compacted version.
If the user requests a preview first, show the full compacted draft and ask again before writing.
If the user stops, make no edits.

substep.title: Verify Result
substep.action: Re-read the written file.
Verify no routine work-log section remains.
Verify all user inputs, decisions, rationale, constraints, unresolved questions, and required references from the signal ledger remain.
Report only the result, target path, and any residual risks.

halt-conditions.condition: No shape path or plan directory provided after asking.
Target file does not exist or is not markdown.
User chooses to stop without editing.
Proposed deletion includes uncertain signal and the user does not approve the risk.
